policy_statement: Prioritize replicable, effective, and up-to-date neighborhood plans and programs for areas with the most potential for change and need of direction.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-1.A: Prioritize replicable, effective, and up-to-date neighborhood plans and programs for those areas with the most potential for change and need of direction.
section_header: GOAL VN-1
extraction_rationale: An explicit policy commitment to prioritize specific planning products (neighborhood plans/programs) for targeted areas.

policy_statement: Inform and engage with neighborhoods and residents during development review, capital improvement planning, and decisions on City/County facilities and services.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-1.B: Inform and engage with neighborhoods, neighborhood-based organizations, and individual residents during the development review process, capital improvement planning, and decisions on City and County facilities and services.
section_header: GOAL VN-1
extraction_rationale: Commits the City to a specific engagement practice across multiple decision processes.

policy_statement: Promote neighborhoods that incorporate common desired neighborhood elements.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-2.A: Promote neighborhoods that incorporate common desired neighborhood elements.
section_header: GOAL VN-2
extraction_rationale: A stated policy direction guiding neighborhood development outcomes (design/land use characteristics).

policy_statement: Preserve and enhance the physical elements that define a neighborhood's character.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-3.A: Preserve and enhance the physical elements that define a neighborhood's character.
section_header: GOAL VN-3
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to preservation/enhancement actions as part of neighborhood reinvestment.

policy_statement: Support unique and innovative community design elements and features such as urban farms and gardens, co-op housing, live/work spaces, and neighborhood gathering places.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-3.B: Support unique and innovative community design elements and features such as urban farms and gardens, co-op housing, live/work spaces, or neighborhood gathering places.
section_header: GOAL VN-3
extraction_rationale: Policy specifies concrete types of supported design/community features, including urban agriculture-related elements.

policy_statement: Promote neighborhood-level shopping and service options to increase local access and walkability (including healthy food markets and other supportive businesses).
verbatim_text: Policy VN-3.C: Promote neighborhood-level shopping and service options to include a variety of healthy food markets, coffee houses, restaurants, and other supportive businesses that increase local access and walkability.
section_header: GOAL VN-3
extraction_rationale: Outcome-oriented policy to promote specific local services to improve access/walkability.

policy_statement: Create strong neighborhood identity through inclusion and integration of arts and culture.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-3.D: Create strong neighborhood identity through the inclusion and integration of arts and culture.
section_header: GOAL VN-3
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy to integrate arts/culture into neighborhood identity—an actionable planning directive.

policy_statement: Encourage and support the integration of mixed-use development in neighborhoods.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-3.E: Encourage and support the integration of mixed-use development in neighborhoods.
section_header: GOAL VN-3
extraction_rationale: Clear land-use policy direction promoting mixed-use development.

policy_statement: Enhance mobility and connectivity between neighborhoods and with surrounding jurisdictions.
verbatim_text: Policy VN-3.F: Enhance mobility and connectivity between neighborhoods across Colorado Springs and with surrounding jurisdictions.
section_header: GOAL VN-3
extraction_rationale: Policy commitment to improve connectivity, supported by strategies on bike lanes/sidewalks/trails.

policy_statement: Emphasize placemaking throughout the city with design and programming that supports a distinctive identity and experience.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-1.A: Emphasize placemaking throughout the city with design and programming that supports a distinctive identity and experience.
section_header: GOAL UP-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy committing to placemaking via design/programming across the city.

policy_statement: Establish a network of connections (gateways, signature streets, festival streets, and trails) to support and define unique places.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-1.B: Establish a network of connections such as gateways, signature streets, festival streets, and trails to support, define, and provide context for our unique places.
section_header: GOAL UP-1
extraction_rationale: Commits to establishing specific connectivity/placemaking elements (gateways/streets/trails).

policy_statement: Locate and design new and repurposed civic facilities as highly visible focal points expressing quality design and community identity.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-1.C: Locate and design new and repurposed civic facilities to make them highly visible focal points that express quality design, permanence, importance, and community identity.
section_header: GOAL UP-1
extraction_rationale: Policy sets a clear design/location direction for civic facilities.

policy_statement: Support infill and land use investment throughout mature and developed areas of the city.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-2.A: Support infill and land use investment throughout the mature and developed areas of the city.
section_header: GOAL UP-2
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to support infill and investment in specified areas.

policy_statement: Proactively participate and invest in Downtown development as the city's premier urban activity center.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-3.A: Proactively participate and invest in the development of Downtown as the city's premier urban activity center.
section_header: GOAL UP-3
extraction_rationale: Includes an investment commitment (participate and invest) tied to Downtown development.

policy_statement: Actively plan and encourage a development pattern of unique centers along new and redeveloped corridors and other designated areas.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-4.A: Actively plan and encourage a development pattern consisting of unique centers located along new and redeveloped corridors and at other designated areas throughout the city.
section_header: GOAL UP-4
extraction_rationale: Specific planning commitment directing development pattern (centers/corridors).

policy_statement: Within unique centers, incorporate density and mixed uses with higher design standards and multimodal access including transit.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-4.B: Within unique centers, incorporate density and mixed uses along with higher standards of design, attention to the public realm, and design for multimodal access including transit.
section_header: GOAL UP-4
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy linking land use density/mixed-use with multimodal/transit-oriented design.

policy_statement: Ensure that the City Zoning Code supports the intent of unique places.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-4.C: Ensure that the City Zoning Code supports the intent of unique places.
section_header: GOAL UP-4
extraction_rationale: Commits to aligning zoning code with the plan’s unique places intent.

policy_statement: Leverage funding tools, partnerships, and policies to fund and maintain redevelopment centers, corridors, and gateways.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-4.D: Leverage funding tools, partnerships, and policies to fund and maintain redevelopment centers, corridors, and gateways.
section_header: GOAL UP-4
extraction_rationale: Resource/funding-oriented policy direction (use of tools/partnerships to fund/maintain).

policy_statement: Actively evaluate plans for urban places and corridors from the perspective of fiscal and environmental sustainability.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-5.A: Actively evaluate plans for existing, new, and redeveloping urban places and corridors from the perspective of fiscal and environmental sustainability.
section_header: GOAL UP-5
extraction_rationale: Policy requires sustainability evaluation (fiscal and environmental) for plans.

policy_statement: Encourage cost-effective development that promotes the wise use of resources.
verbatim_text: Policy UP-5.B: Encourage cost-effective development that promotes the wise use of resources.
section_header: GOAL UP-5
extraction_rationale: Resource-efficiency policy direction for development; supported by strategies on code revisions for energy/water efficiency.

policy_statement: Preserve and strengthen key economic sectors and grow medium- and high-wage jobs in targeted industry clusters.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-1.A: Preserve and strengthen key economic sectors and strive to grow medium and high-wage jobs in targeted industry clusters.
section_header: GOAL TE-1
extraction_rationale: Economic development policy with explicit job-quality focus and targeted clusters.

policy_statement: Build on the city's sports, recreation, and outdoors economy and Olympic City USA brand, including investing in infrastructure and facilities.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-1.B: Build on our emerging sports, recreation, and outdoors economy. This includes the Olympic Training Center, Olympic Museum sports and recreation organizations and other related businesses and industries.
section_header: GOAL TE-1
extraction_rationale: Policy direction to support a specific economic sector; associated strategies include infrastructure/facility investment.

policy_statement: Leverage the city's livability as a workforce and economic driver.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-1.C: Leverage the city's livability as a workforce and economic driver.
section_header: GOAL TE-1
extraction_rationale: Policy sets direction to use livability improvements to attract/retain workforce and investment.

policy_statement: Enhance Cornerstone Institution campuses while integrating them into the surrounding community.
verbatim_text:
Policy TE-1.D: Enhance our Cornerstone Institutions (Typology

1) campuses, while also integrating them into the surrounding community.
section_header: GOAL TE-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit campus development/integration policy affecting land use and community connectivity.

policy_statement: Preserve and strengthen the city's Industry Icons typology (industrial/manufacturing base).
verbatim_text: Policy TE-2.A: Preserve and strengthen the city's Industry Icons Typology (Typology 5).
section_header: GOAL TE-2
extraction_rationale: Direct policy commitment to protect and strengthen industrial employment areas.

policy_statement: Create a positive atmosphere for spinoffs, startups, and entrepreneurship (including supportive land regulations for diverse workplace types).
verbatim_text: Policy TE-2.B: Create a positive atmosphere for spinoffs, startups, and entrepreneurship.
section_header: GOAL TE-2
extraction_rationale: Economic development policy to support entrepreneurship; backed by strategies on adapting processes and land regulations.

policy_statement: Enhance the physical environment by creating new amenities to attract and retain businesses and residents.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-2.C: Enhance the physical environment by creating new amenities that help attract and retain new businesses and residents.
section_header: GOAL TE-2
extraction_rationale: Commits to creating amenities (an intervention) to support economic attraction/retention.

policy_statement: Provide high-quality infrastructure and technology citywide (including expanding internet capacity and redundant utility services).
verbatim_text: Policy TE-2.D: Provide high-quality infrastructure and technology citywide.
section_header: GOAL TE-2
extraction_rationale: Explicit infrastructure/technology policy direction with implementation strategies (internet/utility redundancy/cybersecurity).

policy_statement: Foster cross-jurisdictional collaboration and planning with other public agencies on economic development and fiscal sustainability.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-3.A: Foster cross-jurisdictional collaboration and planning with other public agencies including El Paso County, City of Manitou Springs, City of Fountain, Town of Monument, Pikes Peak Area Council of Governments, Colorado Department of Transportation, and surrounding jurisdictions.
section_header: GOAL TE-3
extraction_rationale: Commits to intergovernmental collaboration as an ongoing policy approach.

policy_statement: Coordinate and partner with regional military installations (including land use consistency near installations).
verbatim_text: Policy TE-3.B: Coordinate and partner with regional military installations.
section_header: GOAL TE-3
extraction_rationale: Policy commitment to coordinate with military installations, affecting planning and development decisions.

policy_statement: Prioritize development within existing City boundaries and built environment rather than the periphery.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-4.A: Prioritize development within the existing City boundaries and built environment (not in the periphery).
section_header: GOAL TE-4
extraction_rationale: Explicit growth-management policy to focus development inward (infill/compact development).

policy_statement: Improve local funding mechanisms to better support economic development efforts.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-4.B: Improve local funding mechanisms to better support economic development efforts.
section_header: GOAL TE-4
extraction_rationale: Funding mechanism policy (resource allocation/financial tools) supporting economic development.

policy_statement: Promote sustainable efforts and initiatives in the public and private sector, including renewable energy, clean technology, waste reduction, and recycling.
verbatim_text:
Policy TE-4.C: Promote sustainable efforts and initiatives in the public and private sector.

Strategy TE-4.C-2: Promote the development of businesses, jobs, and programs focused on renewable energy, clean technology, waste reduction, and recycling.
section_header: GOAL TE-4
extraction_rationale: Explicit sustainability policy with clear climate-relevant action areas (renewables/clean tech/waste reduction/recycling).

policy_statement: Create a plan for a Smart City future.
verbatim_text: Policy TE-5.A: Create a plan for a Smart City future.
section_header: GOAL TE-5
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment to produce a Smart City plan.

policy_statement: Implement Smart City innovations and technology (beginning with pilot projects and scaling proven applications).
verbatim_text:
Policy TE-5.B: Implement Smart City innovations and technology.

Strategy TE-5.B-1: Test prioritized Smart Cities ideas beginning with pilot projects.

Strategy TE-5.B-2: Implement proven and cost-effective Smart Cities applications and technology.
section_header: GOAL TE-5
extraction_rationale: Policy and supporting strategies commit to piloting and implementing Smart City technologies.

policy_statement: Plan for and implement transportation projects to improve overall mobility and manage the impacts of cars on the built environment.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-1.A: Plan for and implement transportation projects for the overall mobility of people and to manage the impacts of cars on our built environment.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Direct commitment to plan and implement transportation projects with specified intent.

policy_statement: Proactively consider and incorporate senior mobility needs in transportation planning and project decisions.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-1.B: Proactively consider and incorporate the needs of our senior population in all relevant transportation planning and project decisions.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy requiring incorporation of senior needs into transportation decisions.

policy_statement: Improve transportation connectivity to the Denver metro area through I-25 capacity expansion, including support for I-25 Gap improvements.
verbatim_text:
Policy SC-1.C: Improve transportation connectivity to the Denver metro area through I-25 capacity expansion.

Strategy SC-1.C-1: Continue to advocate for and support funding and completion of the I-25 Gap improvements.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Policy plus a concrete funded project advocacy commitment (I-25 Gap).

policy_statement: Establish and maintain convenient multimodal connections between neighborhoods, destinations, employment/activity centers, and Downtown.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-1.D: Establish and maintain convenient multimodal connections between neighborhoods, local destinations, employment and activity centers, and Downtown.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit multimodal connectivity commitment supported by numerous implementation strategies.

policy_statement: Develop a Smart City plan that includes comprehensive recommendations for multimodal projects and priorities, including creating and implementing a long-term Electric Vehicle (EV) Plan.
verbatim_text:
Policy SC-1.E: Develop a 'Smart City' plan that includes comprehensive recommendations for multimodal projects and priorities the City can implement over time.

Strategy SC-1.E-5: Create and implement a long-term Electric Vehicle (EV) Plan in collaboration with Colorado Springs Utilities and other stakeholders.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Policy commits to Smart City mobility planning; strategy explicitly commits to creating and implementing an EV Plan.

policy_statement: Maximize multimodal transportation options serving Downtown to reduce the need for individual car ownership and use.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-1.F: Maximize multimodal transportation options serving the Downtown area to reduce the need for individual car ownership and use by visitors, residents, and employees.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy with clear objective (reduce car ownership/use) through multimodal options.

policy_statement: Reduce barriers and negative impacts of major transportation corridors while maintaining needed capacity and function.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-1.G: Reduce the barrier and other negative impacts of major transportation corridors without unreasonably reducing their capacity and function.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Policy directive to mitigate corridor impacts, supported by strategies on crossings and retrofits.

policy_statement: Improve neighborhood livability by connecting neighborhoods to Neighborhood Centers and reducing vehicular speeds.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-1.H: Improve neighborhood livability by providing connections between neighborhoods and Neighborhood Centers (Chapter 3: Typology 1) and by reducing vehicular speeds.
section_header: GOAL SC-1
extraction_rationale: Concrete transportation/urban design policy aiming at speed management and local connections.

policy_statement: Systematically support and encourage the density and design needed for robust transit, beginning with Downtown, key corridors, activity centers, and trip generators.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-2.A: Systematically support and encourage the density and design needed to support this network beginning with Downtown, key corridors, activity centers, and trip generators.
section_header: GOAL SC-2
extraction_rationale: Transit-supportive land use policy (density/design) tied to specific locations.

policy_statement: Enhance and strategically expand the existing transit system to make it a more viable option.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-2.B: Continue to enhance the existing transit system to make it a more viable option and strategically expand the existing system in response to new needs.
section_header: GOAL SC-2
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy commitment to expand/enhance transit service.

policy_statement: Actively plan for high-speed commuter rail or similar service along the Front Range, including preserving potential rail corridors.
verbatim_text:
Policy SC-2.C: Actively plan for a high-speed commuter rail or similar service along the Front Range.

Strategy SC-2.C-2: Preserve existing and potential railway corridors within the city for possible use as future rail corridors between Colorado Springs and Denver.
section_header: GOAL SC-2
extraction_rationale: Policy and strategy include a concrete protective action (corridor preservation) for future rail.

policy_statement: Design new and redeveloped projects to reduce contributions to regional stormwater flows and improve runoff quality.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-3.A: Design new and redeveloped projects to reduce their contribution to regional stormwater flows and to improve the quality of the runoff that is generated.
section_header: GOAL SC-3
extraction_rationale: Stormwater management policy with clear performance objectives (flow reduction, water quality).

policy_statement: Design stormwater infrastructure as an integral, connected part of development/redevelopment and preserve naturalistic stream features where feasible.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-3.B: Design stormwater infrastructure as an integral and connected part of new development and redeveloping areas and preserve or incorporate naturalistic stream profiles and features where feasible.
section_header: GOAL SC-3
extraction_rationale: Commits to integrated stormwater design and nature-based features where feasible.

policy_statement: Continue to invest in upgrading and maintaining stormwater facilities and infrastructure.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-3.C: Continue to invest in upgrading and maintaining facilities and infrastructure to manage stormwater.
section_header: GOAL SC-3
extraction_rationale: Explicit investment commitment for stormwater infrastructure upgrades/maintenance.

policy_statement: Efficiently use existing utility system capacity.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-4.A: Efficiently use the existing utility system capacity.
section_header: GOAL SC-4
extraction_rationale: Utilities policy focused on efficiency and capacity management (supported by infill/density strategies).

policy_statement: Support Colorado Springs Utilities and other investments in smart technologies to reduce energy consumption.
verbatim_text: Policy SC-4.B: Support CSU and other investments in smart technologies to reduce energy consumption.
section_header: GOAL SC-4
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy to invest/support smart technologies for energy reduction.

policy_statement: Preserve historic and cultural resources and integrate history/heritage themes into design and development.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-1.A: Continue to preserve and advance Colorado Springs' historic and cultural resources and integrate themes and features into design and development that represent Colorado Springs' history and heritage.
section_header: GOAL RC-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit preservation and design-integration policy for historic/cultural resources.

policy_statement: Support existing and new defining institutions for arts and culture.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-2.A: Support existing and new Defining Institutions (Typology 1) for arts and culture.
section_header: GOAL RC-2
extraction_rationale: Direct support commitment for arts/culture institutions.

policy_statement: Promote arts and cultural hubs and focal points as elements of activity centers throughout the city.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-2.B: Promote existing and new arts and cultural hubs, venues, and focal points as elements of activity centers throughout the city.
section_header: GOAL RC-2
extraction_rationale: Policy integrating cultural infrastructure into urban activity centers.

policy_statement: Increase accessibility and awareness of the city's attractions, activities, and resources across all cultural typologies.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-2.C: Increase accessibility and awareness about the city's unique attractions, activities, and resources, across all cultural typologies.
section_header: GOAL RC-2
extraction_rationale: Commitment to actions that increase access/awareness (e.g., marketing, wayfinding tech).

policy_statement: Integrate arts, culture, and education as part of the planning process.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-3.A: Integrate arts, culture, and education as part of the planning process.
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Explicit requirement to incorporate arts/culture/education in planning.

policy_statement: Reinforce Downtown as an historic, cultural, and entertainment center of the community and region.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-3.B: Reinforce Downtown as an historic, cultural, and entertainment center of the community and the region.
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Policy direction focusing on Downtown’s role, supported by strategies including Experience Downtown implementation and activating vacant storefronts.

policy_statement: Encourage creation and evolution of creative districts and corridors through support and attraction of museums, performance venues, and galleries.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-3.C: Encourage the creation and evolution of Creative Districts and Corridors (Typology 4) throughout the city through support and attraction of additional museums, performance venues, and galleries for the fine and performance arts.
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Policy commits to expanding creative districts/corridors with specific cultural facilities.

policy_statement: Integrate arts into the natural landscape with outdoor concert venues and public art in parks and along greenways.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-3.D: Integrate the arts into the natural landscape with outdoor concert venues and public art in parks and along greenways to support Pop-Up Culture (Typology 6).
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Actionable policy linking arts programming/infrastructure with parks/greenways.

policy_statement: Encourage adaptability of public rights-of-way and public gathering space to accommodate events and exhibits (including festival streets).
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-3.E: Encourage the adaptability of public rights-of-way and public gathering space to accommodate a wide variety of events and exhibits.

Strategy RC-3.E-3: Designate festival streets within the Unique Places typologies around the city that can be temporarily closed or otherwise repurposed for special events.
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Policy and strategy provide specific intervention: designate festival streets and adapt ROW for events.

policy_statement: Support creative placemaking and environments for artistic expression within creative districts and corridors (including partnerships for artist housing and studio space).
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-3.F: Support creative placemaking and environments for artistic expression within Creative Districts and Corridors.

Strategy RC-3.F-1: Continue partnerships with local and state organizations like ArtSpace to develop attainable housing and studio space for local artists, similar to the Space to Create Colorado initiative.
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Policy plus concrete implementation via partnerships to develop attainable housing/studio space for artists.

policy_statement: Empower neighborhoods to add music, art, and culture to community events (via neighborhood plans).
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-3.G: Empower neighborhoods to add music, art, and culture to their communities and community events.

Strategy RC-3.G-1: Encourage neighborhood-specific arts, culture, and educational opportunities in Neighborhood Plans.
section_header: GOAL RC-3
extraction_rationale: Policy direction to enable/encourage neighborhood-level cultural programming through planning.

policy_statement: Recognize and incentivize local and regional food production/procurement, including creating a city-run recognition program and adopting procurement rules encouraging local/regional vendors.
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-4.A: Recognize and incentivize the production and procurement of local and regional food as fundamental to our city's identity as a Healthy Eating & Active Living (HEAL) city.

Strategy RC-4.A-1: Create a city-run recognition program that highlights restaurants, stores, and institutions that utilize and sell local and regional food products.

Strategy RC-4.A-2: Adopt procurement rules that encourage the use of local and regional food vendors for city contract.
section_header: GOAL RC-4
extraction_rationale: Contains concrete program creation and procurement rule changes tied to local food systems.

policy_statement: Encourage culinary events and pop-up food culture (including continuing zoning and code supportive of food trucks and farmers markets).
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-4.B: Encourage culinary events, collectives, and other food-related Pop-Up Culture that celebrate local chefs and food artisans.

Strategy RC-4.B-1: Continue to provide zoning and other Code regulations supportive of both temporary and more permanent locations for food trucks, and farmers markets throughout the city.
section_header: GOAL RC-4
extraction_rationale: Policy with specific regulatory mechanism (zoning/code support) for food trucks and farmers markets.

policy_statement: Support options for healthy and locally produced food, including zoning that increases access to local food and supports urban agriculture.
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-4.C: Support options for healthy and locally produced food.

Strategy RC-4.C-1: Support zoning that increases access to local food and supports urban agriculture.
section_header: GOAL RC-4
extraction_rationale: Includes explicit land-use/zoning intervention to support urban agriculture and food access.

policy_statement: Build on collaboration with philanthropic foundations, nonprofits, and faith-based groups to connect residents with resources and activities.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-5.A: Build on the momentum of local organizations to connect residents with community-based resources and activities.
section_header: GOAL RC-5
extraction_rationale: Policy commitment to partner/collaborate with community organizations.

policy_statement: Maintain and financially support the sustainability of arts and cultural programming, facilities, and initiatives of community assets.
verbatim_text: Policy RC-5.B: Maintain and financially support the sustainability of arts and cultural programming, facilities and initiatives of our Community Assets (Typology 2).
section_header: GOAL RC-5
extraction_rationale: Explicit financial support commitment for arts/cultural programming and facilities.

policy_statement: Reinforce defining institutions and community assets as hubs of educational activity (including partnerships with higher learning institutions).
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-6.A: Reinforce the city's Defining Institutions and Community Assets as hubs of educational activity.

Strategy RC-6.A-1: Partner with the city's institutions of higher learning on their plans for growth, adaptation, and reinvestment.
section_header: GOAL RC-6
extraction_rationale: Policy and strategy commit to partnerships and planning support for education hubs.

policy_statement: Support a network of public resources and community assets to address educational/training needs and function as places for community activity (including reviewing/approving plans to enhance neighborhood integration).
verbatim_text:
Policy RC-6.B: Support a network of public resources, schools, community centers and other Community Assets that address the city's educational and training needs and also function as places for community activity.

Strategy RC-6.B-1: Review and approve plans for new or redeveloped educational facilities and other Community Assets (Typology 2) to enhance integration within the surrounding neighborhood with an emphasis on access connectivity.
section_header: GOAL RC-6
extraction_rationale: Commits to supporting and approving educational/community facilities in ways that improve integration and access connectivity.

policy_statement: Ensure adequate and sustainable funding for parks, recreation, urban forest, and open space assets.
verbatim_text: Policy ML-1.A: Ensure adequate and sustainable funding for parks, recreation, urban forest, and open space assets.
section_header: GOALS AND POLICIES / PROVIDE PARKS, TREES, AND TRAILS FOR THE PEOPLE / GOAL ML-1
extraction_rationale: A formal policy statement committing to ensuring sustainable funding for parks/urban forest/open space; accompanied by multiple implementation strategies.

policy_statement: Establish coordinated, dedicated, permanent, and sustainable funding sources for parks/forestry/trails/streetscapes/open space acquisition, development, and ongoing maintenance.
verbatim_text: Strategy ML-1.A-1: Establish coordinated, dedicated, permanent, and sustainable sources to adequately fund parks, forestry, trails, streetscape, and open space property acquisition, development, and ongoing maintenance.
parent_policy_name: Ensure adequate and sustainable funding for parks, recreation, urban forest, and open space assets.
section_header: GOALS AND POLICIES / PROVIDE PARKS, TREES, AND TRAILS FOR THE PEOPLE / GOAL ML-1
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention to create dedicated funding sources.

policy_statement: Consider increasing the TOPS (Trails Open Space and Parks) sales tax above the current 0.10 percent.
verbatim_text: Strategy ML-1.A-2: Specifically consider an increase of the TOPS (Trails Open Space and Parks) sales tax from the current 0.10 percent.
parent_policy_name: Ensure adequate and sustainable funding for parks, recreation, urban forest, and open space assets.
section_header: GOALS AND POLICIES / PROVIDE PARKS, TREES, AND TRAILS FOR THE PEOPLE / GOAL ML-1
extraction_rationale: Explicit fiscal measure (tax rate change) to support funding.

policy_statement: Use partnerships, grants, private donations, and businesses to leverage revenue sources and fund priority parks, streetscape, open space, recreation, and trail projects.
verbatim_text: Strategy ML-1.A-3: Use partnerships, grants, private donations, and businesses to leverage dedicated revenue sources and fund strategic parks, facility adoption programs, streetscape, open space, recreation, and trails priority projects.
parent_policy_name: Ensure adequate and sustainable funding for parks, recreation, urban forest, and open space assets.
section_header: GOALS AND POLICIES / PROVIDE PARKS, TREES, AND TRAILS FOR THE PEOPLE / GOAL ML-1
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation approach leveraging partnerships and financing.

policy_statement: Evaluate and implement fee-based and other revenue options for City parks and recreation facilities while retaining equitable access.
verbatim_text: Strategy ML-1.A-4: Evaluate and implement additional opportunities for fee-based and other options to generate revenue from City parks and recreation facilities while retaining equitable access.
parent_policy_name: Ensure adequate and sustainable funding for parks, recreation, urban forest, and open space assets.
section_header: GOALS AND POLICIES / PROVIDE PARKS, TREES, AND TRAILS FOR THE PEOPLE / GOAL ML-1
extraction_rationale: Resource-allocation mechanism via fees; includes equity condition.

policy_statement: Optimize sustainable on- and off-street access to Pike National Forest and other Natural Resources and Regional Recreation areas for all residents and visitors.
verbatim_text: Policy ML-1.B: Optimize sustainable on and off-street access to Pike National Forest and other Natural Resources and Regional Recreation areas (Typology 3) for all residents and visitors.
section_header: GOALS AND POLICIES / PROVIDE PARKS, TREES, AND TRAILS FOR THE PEOPLE / GOAL ML-1
extraction_rationale: Formal policy to optimize access; followed by multiple strategies specifying coordination and trail connectivity actions.

policy_statement: Coordinate physical planning with managers of regional parks and open space (county, state parks, U.S. Forest Service, military installations).

These facilities should complement and add value to our parks and recreation system, and increase opportunities for city residents.

extraction_rationale: Specific access/connectivity intervention.

policy_statement: Utilize, maintain, and expand open space and natural areas along creeks for stormwater conveyance while meeting recreation, education, and transportation needs.

policy_statement: Implement efforts like the Pikes Peak Regional Greenway Master Plan and Fountain Creek Restoration Plan on other stream corridors to improve stormwater management and reduce flooding while optimizing other benefits.

policy_statement: Increase use of xeric and native plants and replace some high-water turf with lower-water native grass species to reduce water demand and maintenance costs.

Reduce the water demand footprint and maintenance costs in parks by identifying high water use turf areas not suitable for recreation and replacing a percentage of these areas with lower-water requiring native grass species.

policy_statement: Recalculate the city’s future development capacity analysis periodically (at least every five years) using a recalibrated methodology reflecting emerging trends, patterns, and conditions.
verbatim_text: Therefore, it is recommended that this capacity analysis be recalculated on a periodic basis (at least every five years), using a recalibrated methodology that best reflects emerging trends, pattern and conditions.
section_header: CAPACITY CALCULATIONS METHODOLOGY
extraction_rationale: This is an explicit recommendation with a defined minimum frequency (“at least every five years”) and a specified action (recalculate the capacity analysis using a recalibrated methodology).
